title: 10 best herbal teas for fever

remedies.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with fever because it promotes sweating, which is the body's natural way of cooling down and releasing toxins. The herb contains anti-inflammatory and immune-boosting properties that help reduce inflammation and strengthen the immune system, thereby helping to fight off infections that can cause fever. Yarrow tea also has a diaphoretic effect, which increases blood circulation to the skin, further aiding in the reduction of fever. Additionally, it is known to stimulate the production of white blood cells, which play a crucial role in defending the body against foreign invaders. However, it is important to note that while yarrow tea can help alleviate symptoms of fever, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe or persistent cases.
remedy_recipe: To make yarrow tea for fever, start by boiling 1 cup of water (240 mL) in a pot, then add 2 teaspoons (10 mL) of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Allow the mixture to steep, covered, for 10 minutes to help extract the medicinal compounds and essential oils.
Strain the tea to remove the plant material, and discard the used yarrow.
For added flavor and additional health benefits, consider adding a slice of lemon and a teaspoon of honey to your yarrow tea.
Drink the tea while still warm, up to three times a day, as needed to help reduce fever and promote sweating.

remedies.remedy_desc: Elderflower tea helps with fever because it contains several compounds with antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. These compounds work together to help reduce fever by promoting perspiration, which helps the body eliminate toxins and cool down. Additionally, elderflower tea is known to stimulate the immune system, aiding in the body's overall response to infection and fever. The antioxidants present in elderflower tea also help protect cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to a faster recovery. Lastly, elderflower tea has a mild diaphoretic effect, increasing blood circulation to the skin and promoting the elimination of waste products, further supporting the body's efforts to reduce fever.
remedy_recipe: To make elderflower tea for fever, begin by boiling 2 cups of water in a pot, then add 2 tablespoons of dried elderflowers or 4-5 fresh elderflower heads; let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
After simmering, remove the pot from heat and strain the tea to remove any flower parts or debris.
Optional: For added flavor and additional health benefits, add 1 tablespoon of honey and a squeeze of lemon juice to the tea.
Stir the honey and lemon juice into the tea until fully combined and let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Serve the warm elderflower tea, sipping it slowly throughout the day to help soothe fever symptoms and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Peppermint tea helps with fever because it contains menthol, a natural organic compound that can help cool down the body by promoting perspiration, which in turn helps reduce fever and cleanses toxins from the body. Additionally, peppermint tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with fever, while its soothing and calming effects can help relax the body and mind, supporting the body's natural healing processes. Furthermore, peppermint tea has been shown to boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections and reduce fever symptoms. However, it's important to note that while peppermint tea can help alleviate fever symptoms, it's not a substitute for medical treatment, and anyone experiencing a high fever or persistent symptoms should seek medical attention.
remedy_recipe: In a tea infuser or empty tea bag, combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried peppermint leaves (adjust to personal taste) and place it in a cup or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool down for about 2-3 minutes to ensure it's not too hot, as very hot water can destroy the beneficial compounds in peppermint.
Pour the heated water over the peppermint leaves in the teapot or cup and let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the release of peppermint's medicinal properties.
Remove the tea infuser or tea bag to prevent over-steeping and bitterness.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and enjoy this soothing, potentially fever-reducing peppermint tea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginger tea helps with fever because it contains natural ingredients that promote sweat production, which is the body's way of cooling down and releasing toxins. The antiviral and antibacterial properties of ginger also help fight off infections that may be causing the fever. Additionally, ginger has anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate the symptoms associated with fever, such as headaches and body aches. Furthermore, ginger tea can help rehydrate the body, which is important when fighting off a fever. Lastly, ginger's warming properties can help improve blood circulation, thereby promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_recipe: Peel and grate 1 tablespoon of fresh ginger, then combine it with 4 cups of water in a saucepan; bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat, add 2 cinnamon sticks and 2 whole cloves, then cover and let the mixture steep for 15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a teapot or a pitcher, discarding the solids; stir in 2 tablespoons of honey and 1 tablespoon of lemon juice.
Warm up 1 cup of the ginger tea, then slowly sip it while still hot, adding more honey or lemon to taste if needed.
Repeat step 4 every 2-3 hours, or as directed by your healthcare provider, to help alleviate fever and soothe symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Holy basil tea helps with fever because it contains therapeutic properties that work to reduce fever by promoting sweating, which is the body's natural way of cooling down. The tea's anti-microbial and anti-inflammatory properties also help in fighting off infections that may be causing the fever. Additionally, holy basil tea is known to boost the immune system, which aids in preventing further illnesses. Furthermore, it helps to alleviate symptoms associated with fever, such as headaches and sore throat, by providing a calming and soothing effect. Lastly, holy basil tea has been used in traditional medicine for centuries as a natural remedy for various ailments, including fever, making it a popular and trusted choice for many people.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, add 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil, then add 1 teaspoon of dried holy basil leaves or 2.5 teaspoons of fresh holy basil leaves and let it simmer for 5 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the tea steep for another 5 minutes, then strain the tea to remove the leaves.
Optional: add honey or lemon to taste for added flavor and potential additional health benefits.
Drink the tea while still warm, up to three times a day, as needed to help reduce fever and soothe symptoms.
Discard any remaining tea that has not been consumed within 24 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Clove tea helps with fever because it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with fever. The main active compound in cloves, eugenol, is thought to be responsible for these effects. Additionally, clove tea can help stimulate sweating, which can help cool the body and reduce fever. Furthermore, cloves have been used traditionally for their antimicrobial properties, which may help fight off infections that could be causing the fever. However, it's important to note that while clove tea can be a helpful addition to fever treatment, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe cases.
remedy_recipe: To make clove tea for fever, start by boiling 1 cup of water and adding 3-5 whole cloves (or 1/2 teaspoon of ground cloves) to the water; allow the mixture to simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the clove-infused water to remove the solid clove particles and retain the liquid tea.
Optional: Add honey or lemon to taste for improved flavor and additional health benefits.
Let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature, ensuring it's not too hot to consume.
Drink 2-3 cups of this clove tea daily, while rest and other self-care measures are also essential for fever management.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tea helps with fever because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, contributing to fever reduction. Additionally, cinnamon has antimicrobial properties that can help fight off infections, which are a common cause of fevers. The warm temperature of the tea can also help promote sweating, which can help cool down the body and reduce fever. Furthermore, cinnamon tea is rich in antioxidants that can help boost the immune system and aid in the body's natural defense against illness. Lastly, cinnamon tea can provide hydration, which is essential for feverish individuals as fever can lead to dehydration, worsening the condition.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, bring 1 cup of water to a boil, then add 1 cinnamon stick or 1 teaspoon of ground cinnamon and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat and optionally add 1 teaspoon of honey or lemon juice for added flavor and vitamin C.
Stir in 1 teaspoon of freshly grated ginger or 1/2 teaspoon of ground ginger for its additional fever-reducing properties.
Cover the pot and let the tea steep for 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld together.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the solids, and enjoy your homemade cinnamon tea for fever.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lemongrass tea helps with fever because it contains anti-pyretic properties that work to reduce fever by bringing down the body's elevated temperature. Additionally, it has anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects that help alleviate discomfort and pain associated with fever. Furthermore, lemongrass tea is rich in vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C and magnesium, which support the immune system and promote faster recovery. Its diaphoretic properties also help stimulate perspiration, which aids in eliminating toxins and excess heat from the body. Lastly, lemongrass tea's calming and soothing effects can help promote rest and relaxation, which are crucial for a speedy recovery.
remedy_recipe: To make lemongrass tea for fever, start by bringing 4 cups of water to a boil, then add 3 stalks of fresh lemongrass (bruised and cut into 2-inch pieces) and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
After simmering, remove the pot from heat and optionally add 1-2 tablespoons of honey and a squeeze of lime juice for added flavor and vitamin C.
Stir the honey and lime juice into the tea until fully dissolved.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a teapot or directly into teacups to remove the lemongrass pieces.
Serve the lemongrass tea while warm, and enjoy its soothing and potentially fever-reducing properties. Drink 2-3 times a day for best results.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea tea helps with fever because it contains several active compounds, such as alkamides, cichoric acid, and echinacosides, that have been shown to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation. These compounds work together to help the body fight off infections, which can cause fever. Additionally, echinacea tea has been found to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help to lower fever and alleviate other symptoms of illness. Furthermore, echinacea tea has been shown to increase the production of white blood cells, which are crucial for fighting off infections and reducing fever. However, it's important to note that echinacea tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, and anyone with a fever should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedy_recipe: To make echinacea tea for fever, start by boiling 1 cup of water in a pot, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried echinacea flowers and/or leaves, reducing heat to a simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the used echinacea material.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, for added flavor and to soothe a sore throat.
Drink the tea while warm, up to three times a day, as needed to help alleviate fever and boost the immune system.
For best results, continue to drink echinacea tea for several days, or as directed by a healthcare provider.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomile tea helps with fever because it contains several chemical components, such as bisabolol and matricin, that possess anti-inflammatory and antiphlogistic properties. These compounds help in reducing inflammation and swelling, which in turn, assist the body in fighting off infections that may be causing the fever. Moreover, chamomile tea is known for its mild sedative effects, which can help alleviate the discomfort and restlessness often associated with fevers. Additionally, chamomile has been found to promote perspiration, which can help cool the body and reduce feverish symptoms. However, it is essential to note that chamomile tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe fever cases, and it is always best to consult a healthcare professional for proper evaluation and guidance.
remedy_recipe: Begin by boiling 8 ounces of water and, while waiting, measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried chamomile flowers or 1 chamomile tea bag.
Once the water has reached a boil, pour it over the chamomile flowers or tea bag in a heat-resistant container or mug.
Allow the chamomile to steep in the hot water for 5-10 minutes, depending on how strong you prefer your tea.
Remove the chamomile flowers or tea bag, and sweeten the tea with honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the chamomile tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day, to help alleviate fever and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Licorice root tea helps with fever because it contains substances like glycyrrhizin, which can help to stimulate the production of anti-inflammatory hormones in the body. These hormones can help to reduce the symptoms of fever, including inflammation and high temperature. Additionally, licorice root tea can help to soothe a sore throat and ease congestion, which are common symptoms that can accompany a fever. Furthermore, licorice root has been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help to fight off infections that may be causing the fever. However, it's important to note that while licorice root tea can help to alleviate fever symptoms, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe or persistent cases. As with any natural remedy, it's always best to consult with a healthcare provider before use.
remedy_recipe: To make licorice root tea for fever, bring 4 cups of water to a boil in a pot, then add 1-2 tablespoons of dried licorice root and let it simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup or mug using a fine-mesh sieve or a strainer to remove the licorice root pieces.
Optional: Add honey or lemon to taste for added flavor and potential additional benefits.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day, as needed to help reduce fever and soothe a sore throat.
Discard any leftover tea after 24 hours, and store any remaining dried licorice root in a cool, dry, and dark place for future use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hyssop tea helps with fever because it has long been known for its anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and expectorant properties, which can help the body fight off infections that may be causing the fever. The herb contains compounds like flavonoids, volatile oils, and tannins that work together to stimulate perspiration, helping to cool the body and reduce feverish symptoms. Additionally, hyssop tea is believed to improve circulation, allowing the immune system to function more effectively and further aiding in the body's recovery process. Its diaphoretic properties also promote the release of toxins through the skin, which can help speed up the healing process and alleviate feverish symptoms. However, it is important to note that hyssop tea should not be consumed in large quantities or by individuals with certain medical conditions, such as pregnancy or epilepsy, as it can have adverse effects.
remedy_recipe: To make hyssop tea for fever, start by boiling 1 cup of water in a pot, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried hyssop leaves or 2-3 teaspoons of fresh hyssop leaves, and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, and discard the used hyssop.
If desired, add honey or lemon to taste for added flavor and potential additional benefits.
Drink the tea while still warm, up to three times a day, as needed to help reduce fever and soothe symptoms.
Monitor your temperature and symptoms, and if they persist for more than a few days or worsen, seek medical attention.

remedies.remedy_desc: Catnip tea helps with fever because it contains nepetalactone, a compound that is believed to have fever-reducing properties. This compound is thought to cause a cooling effect on the skin, which can help reduce body temperature. Additionally, catnip has been historically used as a natural remedy for various ailments, including fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and sedative effects. However, it's important to note that while catnip tea may provide some relief, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in cases of high fever or fever accompanied by other symptoms. It's always best to consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. Lastly, it's worth mentioning that more research is needed to fully understand the mechanisms behind catnip's fever-reducing effects and its potential therapeutic uses.
remedy_recipe: To make catnip tea for fever, start by boiling 1 cup of water and adding 1-2 teaspoons of dried catnip leaves, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the catnip leaves, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, for added flavor and potential additional health benefits.
Drink 2-3 cups of this tea daily, as needed, to help reduce fever and promote sweating.
Monitor your temperature and consult with a healthcare professional if your fever persists for more than 3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with fever because it has antipyretic and anti-inflammatory properties that work to reduce fever by cooling the body and fighting off the underlying infection. The tea's warm nature stimulates perspiration, which helps eliminate toxins and cools the body down. Fennel tea is also known to boost the immune system, which aids in fighting off infections that can cause fever. Additionally, fennel contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and soothe irritated tissues in the body, further supporting the body's natural healing process. Lastly, fennel tea's calming effect can help alleviate anxiety and promote better sleep, which is essential for a faster recovery.
remedy_recipe: Thoroughly rinse and roughly chop 2 tablespoons of fresh fennel seeds, then place them in a teapot or saucepan and add 16 ounces of freshly boiled water, allowing the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes.

Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve into a mug, discarding the used fennel seeds, and optionally add a small amount of raw honey or lemon juice to taste for added flavor and potential immune support.
Warm the tea in the microwave or on the stovetop, if necessary, to a comfortable drinking temperature, being careful not to overheat it and potentially damage the beneficial compounds in the fennel.
Slowly sip the tea, taking care to relax and breathe deeply, allowing the warmth and aroma of the fennel to soothe and comfort you as it helps to alleviate fever symptoms.

Repeat this process every few hours, as needed, for up to several days, ensuring that you stay hydrated and monitor your symptoms, seeking medical attention if your fever persists for more than a few days or is accompanied by severe or worsening symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with fever because it has anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and ant viral properties that work together to reduce fever and soothe symptoms. The rosmarinic acid and safficinolide found in sage tea can help inhibit the production of inflammatory chemicals in the body, which in turn helps lower fever and alleviate discomfort. Additionally, sage tea is known to stimulate the sweat glands, promoting perspiration and helping the body eliminate toxins, further aiding in the reduction of fever. Furthermore, its antimicrobial properties can help fight off any underlying infections that may be causing the fever. Lastly, sage tea's calming effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are often associated with fevers, providing overall comfort and well-being.
remedy_recipe: To make sage tea for fever, first boil 1 cup of water in a saucepan, then add 1-2 teaspoons of fresh sage leaves or 1 teaspoon of dried sage leaves, reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Next, turn off the heat and let the tea cool down for a few minutes, then strain the liquid to remove the sage leaves.
For added flavor and additional health benefits, consider adding a slice of lemon and a teaspoon of honey to the tea.
Stir the tea until the honey is fully dissolved, then drink it while it's still warm, taking small sips to soothe your throat and help reduce fever.
Drink 2-3 cups of sage tea per day, as needed, to help alleviate fever symptoms, but discontinue use if symptoms persist for more than a few days or worsen.

remedies.remedy_desc: Marshmallow root tea helps with fever because it contains mucilage, a substance that coats the throat and stomach, providing a soothing effect. This coating can also help to reduce inflammation, which can help to lower fever. Additionally, marshmallow root has been found to have expectorant properties, helping to expel excess mucus from the respiratory tract, which can contribute to fever reduction. Furthermore, it may have immune-boosting properties, helping the body to fight off infections that can cause fever. However, it's important to note that while marshmallow root tea can be a helpful addition to fever management, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe or persistent cases.
remedy_recipe: To make marshmallow root tea for fever, bring 1 quart of water to a boil, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried marshmallow root and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
After simmering, turn off the heat and let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Strain the tea to remove the marshmallow root pieces, then add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 2-3 cups of this tea daily, as needed, to help soothe fever symptoms.
Store any leftover tea in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, reheating as necessary.

remedies.remedy_desc: Raspberry leaf tea helps with fever because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with fever. It also contains tannins and flavonoids, which have been shown to help stimulate sweating, allowing the body to cool down and release toxins. Additionally, raspberry leaf tea is known for its ability to boost the immune system, helping the body fight off infections that may be causing the fever. Furthermore, it can help soothe a sore throat and alleviate other symptoms associated with fever, such as headaches and body aches. However, it's important to note that while raspberry leaf tea can help alleviate symptoms of fever, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe cases.
remedy_recipe: To make raspberry leaf tea for fever, start by boiling 1 cup of water and adding 1-2 teaspoons of dried raspberry leaves (adjust amount based on desired strength); allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes.
After steeping, strain the tea to remove the raspberry leaves, ensuring a smooth and enjoyable drinking experience.
For added flavor and additional health benefits, consider adding a squeeze of lemon or a small amount of honey to your raspberry leaf tea.
To serve, pour the tea into a cup or mug, making sure it's not too hot to drink; allow it to cool slightly if necessary.
Enjoy your homemade raspberry leaf tea as a soothing, fever-reducing beverage, and feel free to drink multiple cups throughout the day as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Alfalfa tea helps with fever because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with fever. Additionally, alfalfa contains vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C and magnesium, that can help boost the immune system and aid in fighting off infections that may be causing the fever. The tea also has natural diuretic properties that can help the body eliminate toxins and reduce fluid retention, which can help lower fever. Furthermore, alfalfa tea has been shown to have a cooling effect on the body, which can directly help reduce fever symptoms. However, it is important to note that while alfalfa tea can help alleviate fever symptoms, it should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment and advice from a healthcare professional.
remedy_recipe: To make alfalfa tea for fever, start by boiling 1 cup of water in a pot, then add 1 teaspoon of dried alfalfa leaves or 1 tablespoon of fresh alfalfa leaves, reduce heat, cover and simmer for 15 minutes.
After simmering, strain the tea to remove the leaves and discard them.
If desired, add honey or lemon to taste for flavor, but avoid adding sugar as it can increase fever.
Drink the tea while still warm, up to three times a day, as needed to help reduce fever and promote sweating.
Monitor your temperature and rest, seeking medical attention if fever persists for more than 3 days or if it rises above 103°F (39.4°C).

remedies.remedy_desc: Burdock root tea helps with fever because it has anti-inflammatory and diaphoretic properties that promote sweating and help reduce feverish symptoms. The tea's active compounds, such as polyacetylenes and tannins, work together to stimulate the immune system, eliminating toxins and reducing inflammation in the body. Moreover, burdock root tea is known for its ability to purify the blood and cool down internal heat, further supporting the body's natural fever-reducing processes. By gently increasing perspiration, burdock root tea helps the body release heat, making it an effective and natural way to alleviate fevers. Additionally, burdock root tea's mild diuretic effect aids in flushing out toxins, providing additional support to the immune system during feverish states.
remedy_recipe: To make burdock root tea for fever, start by bringing 2 cups of water to a boil, then add 2 tablespoons of dried burdock root, reduce heat, and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
After simmering, strain the tea to remove the burdock root pieces, then add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
For best results, cover and let the tea steep for an additional 5-10 minutes to extract maximum beneficial compounds.
Drink the burdock root tea while still warm, up to three times a day, to help reduce fever and promote sweating.
Dispose of any remaining tea after 24 hours and make a fresh batch as needed, as the tea's medicinal properties may diminish over time.

remedies.remedy_desc: Red clover tea helps with fever because it has been traditionally used as an herbal remedy to promote sweating, which is one way the body naturally cools down and eliminates toxins, potentially reducing fever. The tea's diuretic properties may also help in reducing fever by increasing urine production, which can help lower body temperature. Red clover contains several compounds, including flavonoids and essential fatty acids, that may contribute to its fever-reducing effects. However, it is important to note that red clover tea should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe or persistent fever cases. As with any herbal remedy, it is always advisable to consult with a healthcare provider before use.
remedy_recipe: To make red clover tea for fever, first bring 1 cup of water to a boil, then add 1-2 teaspoons of dried red clover flowers and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the flowers, then add honey or lemon to taste for flavor, if desired.
Drink this tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help reduce fever and promote sweating.
Before starting, consult with a healthcare professional if you have any health conditions or are taking any medications, as red clover may interact with certain drugs.
For children, adjust the amount of red clover and water according to their age and weight, and always consult with a pediatrician before giving herbal teas to children.

intro: Herbal teas, particularly those made from ingredients like ginger, peppermint, and chamomile, can help alleviate symptoms of fever due to their anti-inflammatory, diaphoretic, and calming properties. These herbs can help to reduce inflammation in the body, promoting a decrease in fever. Additionally, diaphoretic herbs, like ginger and peppermint, can increase perspiration, which helps the body to cool down and release toxins. Chamomile has calming properties that can help a person relax and get much-needed rest while fighting a fever. However, it's important to note that while herbal teas can provide relief and support the immune system, they should not be used as a substitute for medical treatment, especially in severe or persistent fever cases. Always consult a healthcare professional for proper evaluation and guidance.
